title: DiffReview Walkthrough
description: Guided review walkthrough of a set of changes, consumed by the DiffReview Neovim plugin. Lives at the repository root as .walkthrough.json.

overview.description: Shown before the graph: 1-3 sentences explaining what changed and why.

root.description: Active author-context sentence for the top-level feature, fix, or refactor.

commit.description: Full sha of HEAD at generation time (git rev-parse HEAD). Used for staleness detection; positions describe the working tree as it existed on top of this commit.

tasks.description: Major review tasks. Each task title becomes a numbered review section and Task N.* navigation context.

title.description: Active author-context sentence, e.g. Add..., Update..., Move..., Split..., Remove..., Preserve...

justification.description: Optional intent/rationale shown as yellow italic text directly under the task title; 80 characters or less.

title.description: Concrete owning code boundary such as a module, file, package, directory, crate, or app.
Prose intent row under a group; explains the local purpose served by the concrete items.

justification.description: Optional intent/rationale shown as yellow italic text directly under the subtask title; 80 characters or less.

action.description: Title-cased action verb. Update displays as Modify in the summary.

subtype.description: Optional narrower display label such as Resource or Buffer. Use only when the item's base class, trait, interface, framework role, or semantic kind is known; the summary renders this instead of the base type keyword.

title.description: Story node or concrete code symbol in the expanded graph.

note.description: Short imperative annotation explaining what this node does in the change; 50 characters or less.

groupType.description: Broad container-level construct for group headers. The summary renders this as a lowercase keyword before the group title.

itemType.description: Broad programming construct rendered as a lowercase keyword between the action and item title unless subtype is set. Function and Method both render as fn.

line.description: 1-based line in the NEW (post-change) file as it exists on disk

kind.description: Reviewer-critical callout kind. Deviation is for departures from the original plan; workaround is for a temporary or indirect fix for an issue not fully solved here.

text.description: Compact reviewer-critical context, 180 characters or less. Do not restate the title or comment.

start.description: Start of the referenced region. The line should be a CHANGED (added) line, not unchanged context - reviewers are jumped to it inside the diff.

end.description: Inclusive end of the referenced region; must be >= start. Keep the region tight around the discussed change (roughly 40 lines at most).

comment.description: Mini justification for this step: problem or pressure first, then the solution/change and why it addresses that problem; 1-4 sentences, aiming for roughly 180-200 characters. Do not repeat the file path.

title.description: Succinct active action sentence for the inline step header, e.g. Route API requests through the limiter.

callout.description: Optional singular callout for reviewer-critical context. Most steps omit it.
